name: pysqueezebox

metadata.name: pysqueezebox
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Asynchronous library to control Logitech Media Server

documentation:
# pysqueezebox - Asynchronous control of squeezeboxes
This a library to control a Logitech Media Server asynchronously, intended for
integration with Home Assistant.

Much of the code was adapted from the Home Assistant squeezebox integration.
The current convention is for all API-specific code to be part of a third
party library hosted on PyPi, so I created a separate library.

The function names track the terms used by the LMS API, so they do not all
match the old Home Assistant squeezebox integration.

Thank you to the original author of the squeezebox integration. If it is you,
please let me know so I can credit you here.

# Usage
Install pysqueezebox from github, or using PyPi via pip.
```sh
$ pip3 install pysqueezebox
```

## Imports
Import the Server() and Player() classes from this module. You will also need
to create an aiohttp.ClientSession() that the module will use to communicate
with the Logitech Media Server.

You can use Server.async_get_players() to retrieve a list of connected players,
or get a specific player using Server.async_get_player(name="PlayerName").
Remember that any method starting with "async_" is a coroutine that must be
preceded by an await to run.

For more information on using aiohttp.ClientSession(), see
https://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/client_reference.html.
```Python
from pysqueezebox import Server, Player
import aiohttp
import asyncio
SERVER = '192.168.1.2' # ip address of Logitech Media Server

async def main():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        lms = Server(session, SERVER)
        player = await lms.async_get_player(name="Bedroom")
	await player.async_update()
	print(player.album)
	await player.async_play()

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.run_until_complete(main())
```

## Player.async_update()
The Player object stores information about the current status of the player.
This allows you to retrieve the player's properties without any I/O. Remember
to call Player.async_update() prior to retrieving properties if you want the
most up-to-date information.

## Player() class
Most of the useful functions are in the Player class. More documentation to
follow, but in the meantime, the docstrings should be instructive.

modules.name: pysqueezebox
documentation:
This a library to control a Logitech Media Server asynchronously.

This library is intended for integration with Home Assistant.

Much of the code was adapted from the Home Assistant squeezebox integration.
The current convention is for all API-specific code to be part of a third
party library hosted on PyPi, so I created a separate library.

The function names track the terms used by the LMS API, so they do not all
match the old Home Assistant squeezebox integration.

Thank you to the original author of the squeezebox integration. If it is you,
please let me know so I can credit you here.

(c) 2020 Raj Laud raj.laud@gmail.com

modules.name: pysqueezebox.discovery
documentation: The pysqueezebox server discovery module.

functions.name: pysqueezebox.discovery._unpack_discovery_response
documentation: Return dict of unpacked responses from Logitech Media Server.

functions.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.async_discover
documentation:
Search for Logitech Media Servers using the LMS UDP discovery protocol.

Will search indefinitely. To stop searching, call Task.cancel().

Parameters:
    callback: awaitable or synchronous function to call with Server object
              containing discovered server (required)
    session:  aiohttp.ClientSession for connecting to server (recommended,
              but can be left blank and set by callback instead)

variables.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.DISCOVERY_INTERVAL
documentation: default value from Logitech Media Server code

classes.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.ServerDiscoveryProtocol

methods.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.ServerDiscoveryProtocol.__init__
documentation: Initialize with callback function.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.ServerDiscoveryProtocol.connection_made
documentation: Connect to transport.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.discovery.ServerDiscoveryProtocol.datagram_received
documentation: Test if responder is a Logitech Media Server.
Protocol to send discovery request and receive responses.

modules.name: pysqueezebox.server
documentation: The pysqueezebox.Server() class.

classes.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Logitech device.

Parameters:
    session: aiohttp.ClientSession for connecting to server (required)
    host: LMS server to connect with (required)
    port: LMS server port (optional, default 9000)
    username: LMS username (optional)
    password: LMS password (optional)
    uuid: LMS uuid (optional, will be updated on first async_status() call)
    name: LMS server name (optional, only available through discovery)
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.__repr__
documentation: Return representation of Server object.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_players
documentation:
Return Player for each device connected to LMS.

Parameters:
    search: filter the result by case-insensitive substring (optional)

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_player
documentation:
Return Player for a device connected to server.

Parameters (one required):
    player_id: The unique player_id reported by the server.
    name: A substring for a case-insensitive match. Will return first of
          multiple matching results.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_status
documentation:
Return status of current server.

Without extra parameters the response will have type ServerStatus.

Extra tagged parameters are added to the response dictionary.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_command
documentation: Send a command to the JSON-RPC connection where no result is returned.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_query
documentation: Return result of query on the JSON-RPC connection.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_browse
documentation:
Browse the music library.

Returns a dictionary with the following keys:
    title: item being browsed, e.g., "Artists", "Jimi Hendrix", "Jazz"
    items: list of dictionaries
      title: title of item
      id: unique identifier for item
      image_url (optional): image url if available. will not be set if unavailable

Parameters:
    category: one of playlists, playlist, albums, album, artists, artist, titles,
      genres, genre, favorites, favorite, new music, apps, app, app-cmd
    limit (optional): set maximum number of results
    browse_id (optional): tuple of id type and value
      id type: "album_id", "artist_id", "genre_id", or "track_id"
      value: the id

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_count
documentation: Return number of category in database.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_query_category
documentation: Return list of entries in category, optionally filtered by search string.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_category
documentation: Update cache of library category if needed and return result.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_category_title
documentation: Search of the category name corresponding to a title.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.generate_image_url_from_track_id
documentation: Generate an image url using a track id.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.generate_image_url
documentation: Add the appropriate base_url to a relative image_url.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.get_track_id_from_image_url
documentation: Get a track id from an image url.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.server.Server.async_get_album_id_from_url
documentation: Find the album_id from a favorites url.

Represents a Logitech media server.

Right now, only those features used by the pre-existing Home Assistant
squeezebox integration are implemented.
pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes

classes.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the SqueezeBox device.

Parameters:
    lms: the Server object controlling the player (required)
    player_id: the unique identifier for the player (required)
    name: the player's name (required)
    status: status dictionary for player (optional)
    model: the player's model name (optional)

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.__repr__
documentation: Return representation of Player object.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.name
documentation: Return the name of the device.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.player_id
documentation: Return the player ID, which is its MAC address.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.model
documentation: Return the players model name, e.g. Squeezebox Boom

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.model_type
documentation: Return the players model type, e.g. baby

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.firmware
documentation: Return the player's firmware version if available

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.creator
documentation: Return the player's creator if available

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.connected
documentation:
Return True if the player is connected to the LMS server.

The API call is less useful than it sounds, because after player has
been disconnected for a few minutes from the server, it disappears
altogether from the API. We still return False, not None, because it
still means the player is disconnected.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.power
documentation: Return the power state of the device.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.mode
documentation: Return the mode of the device. One of play, stop, or pause.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.volume
documentation:
Return volume level of the Player.

Returns integer from 0 to 100.
LMS will return a negative integer if the volume is muted. This leads
to inconsistent results if you later try to update the volume with
the negative number, which is instead interpreted as a decrement.
We return the absolute value, separating out volume from muting.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.announce_volume
documentation: Return volume level for announcements.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.muting
documentation: Return true if volume is muted.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.current_title
documentation: Return title of current playing media on remote stream.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.duration
documentation: Return duration of current playing media in seconds.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.duration_float
documentation: Return duration of current playing media in floating point seconds.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.time
documentation:
Return position of current playing media in seconds.

The LMS API calls this "time" so we follow that convention.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.time_float
documentation:
Return position of current playing media in floating point seconds.

The LMS API calls this "time" so we follow that convention.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.image_url
documentation: Return image url of current playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.current_index
documentation: Return the current index in the playlist.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.current_track
documentation: Return playlist_loop or remoteMeta dictionary for current track.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.remote
documentation: Return true if current media is a remote stream.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.remote_title
documentation: Return title of current playing media on remote stream.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.title
documentation: Return title of current playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.artist
documentation: Return artist of current playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.album
documentation: Return album of current playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.content_type
documentation: Return content type of current playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.bitrate
documentation: Return bit rate of current playing media as a string including units.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.samplerate
documentation: Return sample rate of current playing media in KHz, if known.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.samplesize
documentation: Return sample size of current playing media in bits.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.shuffle
documentation: Return shuffle mode. May be 'none, 'song', or 'album'.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.repeat
documentation: Return repeat mode. May be 'none', 'song', or 'playlist'.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.url
documentation: Return the url for the currently playing media.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.playlist
documentation: Return the current playlist.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.alarms
documentation: Return the list of alarms.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.alarms_enabled
documentation: Return the state of the alarms enabled player setting.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.alarm_state
documentation: Return the current alarm state

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.alarm_next
documentation: Return the time stamp of the next alarm (seconds since the epoch)

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.playlist_urls
documentation: Return only the urls of the current playlist. Useful for comparing playlists.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.playlist_tracks
documentation: Return the current playlist length.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.synced
documentation: Return true if currently synced.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.sync_master
documentation: Return the player id of the sync group master.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.sync_slaves
documentation: Return the player ids of the sync group slaves.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.sync_group
documentation: Return the player ids of all players in current sync group.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.create_property_future
documentation:
Create a future awaiting a property value.

prop: the property to test
test: future satisfied when test(prop) returns true. Must accept test(None).
interval: how often to poll, defaults to POLL_INTERVAL but may be set to None for
          passive wait (optional)

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player._wait_for_property
documentation: Wait for property to hit certain state or timeout.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_command
documentation: Send a command to the player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_query
documentation: Return result of a query specific to this player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_update
documentation:
Update the current state of the player.
Also updates the list of alarms set for this player.

Return True if successful, False if update fails.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_volume
documentation: Set volume level, range 0..100, or +/- integer.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.set_announce_volume
documentation: Set the volume level for announcements.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_muting
documentation: Mute (true) or unmute (false) squeezebox.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_toggle_pause
documentation: Send command to player to toggle play/pause.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_play
documentation: Send play command to player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_stop
documentation: Send stop command to player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_pause
documentation: Send pause command to player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player._async_pause_stop
documentation:
Retry pause or stop command until successful or timed out.

Necessary because a pause or stop command sent immediately after a play command will be
silently ignored by LMS.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_index
documentation:
Change position in playlist.

index: if an unsigned integer, change to this position. if preceded by a + or -,
       move forward or backward this many tracks. (required)

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_time
documentation: Seek to a particular time in track.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_power
documentation: Turn on or off squeezebox.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_load_url
documentation:
Play a specific track by url.

cmd: "play" or "load" - replace current playlist (default)
cmd: "play_now" - adds to current spot in playlist
cmd: "insert" - adds next in playlist
cmd: "add" - adds to end of playlist
cmd: "announce" - interrupts the current playlist then resumes it after this track

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_load_playlist
documentation:
Play a playlist, of the sort return by the Player.playlist property.

playlist: an array of dictionaries, which must each have a key
          called "url." (required)
cmd: "play" or "load" - replace current playlist (default)
cmd: "insert" - adds next in playlist
cmd: "add" - adds to end of playlist
cmd: "announce" - interrupts the current playlist then resumes it after this playlist

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_add_alarm
documentation:
Creates a new alarm clock on this player.
Follows the description on http(s)://<server>:<port>/html/docs/cli-api.html?player=#alarm

Parameters
----------
time : datetime.time
    Mandatory time of alarm
dow : list[int]
    Day Of Week. 0 is Sunday, 1 is Monday, etc. up to 6 being Saturday.
    Default: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
enabled : bool
    Default: False
repeat : bool
    Set to True to make this a repeated alarm, False otherwise.
    Default: True
volume : int
    Volume for this alarm, valid values are 0-100, defaults to default alarm volume
url: str
    URL for the alarm playlist, defaults to current playlist

Returns
-------
alarm_id: str
    ID of newly created alarm, None if not successfull

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_update_alarm
documentation:
Updates an existing alarm clock
Follows the description on http(s)://<server>:<port>/html/docs/cli-api.html?player=#alarm

Parameters
----------
alarm_id : str
    Mandatory id of the alarm to update
time : datetime.time
    `time` of alarm
dow : list of ints
    Day Of Week. 0 is Sunday, 1 is Monday, etc. up to 6 being Saturday.
    Default: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6].
enabled : bool
    Default: False
repeat : bool
    True if this is a repeated alarm, False if it runs only one time.
    Default: True
volume : int
    Volume for this alarm, valid values are 0-100, defaults to default alarm volume
url: str
    URL for the alarm playlist, defaults to current playlist

Returns
-------
alarm_id: str
    ID of updated alarm, None if not successful

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_delete_alarm
documentation:
Deletes an existing alarm clock
Follows the description on http(s)://<server>:<port>/html/docs/cli-api.html?player=#alarm

Parameters
----------
alarm_id : str
    Mandatory id of the alarm to delete

Returns
-------
bool
    True if successful, False otherwise

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_shuffle
documentation: Enable/disable shuffle mode.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_repeat
documentation: Enable/disable repeat.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_clear_playlist
documentation: Send the media player the command for clear playlist.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_sync
documentation:
Add another Squeezebox player to this player's sync group.

If the other player is a member of a sync group, it will leave the
current sync group without asking.

Other player may be a player object, or a player_id.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_unsync
documentation: Unsync this Squeezebox player.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_browse
documentation:
Browse the music library.

See Server.async_browse for parameters.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.generate_image_url_from_track_id
documentation: Return the image url for a track_id.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.generate_image_url
documentation: Return the image url.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_save_player_state
documentation: Save the current player state for later restoration.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_restore_player_state
documentation: Restore the player state to the saved state.

methods.name: pysqueezebox.player.Player.async_set_alarms_enabled
documentation: Enable or disable alarms on this player.
Representation of a SqueezeBox device.
